question: By what factor do members who complete their first transaction within 5 days of onboarding show lower first-year churn compared to members who take more than 14 days to transact?

kp_off.answer: According to the reference facts, members who complete their first transaction within 5 days of onboarding show **67% lower first-year churn** compared to members who take more than 14 days to transact.

kp_fixed.answer:
According to the reference facts, members who complete their first transaction within 5 days of onboarding show **67% lower first-year churn** compared to members who take more than 14 days to transact.

This is stated as a percentage reduction, not a multiplicative factor. In other words, early transactors (within 5 days) have churn rates that are 67 percentage points lower than late transactors (14+ days).

question: What surplus reserve threshold must be exceeded before a cooperative platform can distribute profits to its member-owners?

top_k_facts.title: Member Satisfaction NPS Target and Genuinely-Cooperative Band

kp_off.answer:
According to the reference facts, a cooperative platform can distribute surplus to its member-owners when reserves reach **110% of the reserve floor**.

This is specified in **EG-20: Surplus Distribution Trigger Threshold**, which states that 110% of the reserve floor triggers surplus distribution.

top_k_facts.title: Member Satisfaction NPS Target and Genuinely-Cooperative Band
Time to First Transaction and Early-Cohort Churn Reduction

kp_fixed.answer:
Based on the provided reference facts, a cooperative platform can distribute surplus to member-owners when reserves reach **110% of the reserve floor**.

This is specified in **EG-20: Surplus Distribution Trigger Threshold**, which states that 110% of the reserve floor triggers surplus distribution. According to the fact, this threshold ensures that the reserve base is adequately secured before surplus is redirected to members, reducing the risk of losing consolidated gains.

top_k_facts.title: Member Satisfaction NPS Target and Genuinely-Cooperative Band
Time to First Transaction and Early-Cohort Churn Reduction

kp_gamma.answer:
Based on the provided reference facts, the surplus reserve threshold that must be exceeded before a cooperative platform can distribute profits to its member-owners is:

**110% of the reserve floor**

According to EG-20 (Surplus Distribution Trigger Threshold), surplus distribution is triggered when reserves reach 110% of the reserve floor. This means the platform must first secure reserves to 110% of the minimum defensible standard before redistributing surplus to members as economic surplus.

question: What is the canonical amendment supermajority threshold required for constitutional changes in the Verdania cooperative framework?

kp_off.answer: According to the reference facts, the canonical amendment supermajority threshold for constitutional changes in the Verdania cooperative framework is **66.7%** (CS-03: Amendment Supermajority Threshold).

top_k_facts.title: Account Inactivity Warning Threshold and AI Governance Participation Multiplier

kp_fixed.answer:
Based on the provided reference facts, the canonical amendment supermajority threshold for constitutional changes in the Verdania cooperative framework is **66.7%**.

This is specified in CS-03: Amendment Supermajority Threshold, which identifies 66.7% as the supermajority threshold for constitutional amendments in cooperative networks.

top_k_facts.title: Account Inactivity Warning Threshold and AI Governance Participation Multiplier

kp_gamma.answer:
According to the reference facts, the canonical amendment supermajority threshold for constitutional changes in the Verdania cooperative framework is **66.7%**.

This is specified in CS-03: Amendment Supermajority Threshold, which identifies 66.7% as the supermajority threshold for constitutional amendments within the zone of legitimate protection (60-75%) against both excessively low thresholds that allow transient majorities to rewrite foundational agreements and excessively high thresholds that entrench outdated provisions.
